rulings.Case Title: T&C PROPERTIES, LLC VS. ST. JAMES PROPERTY CO., LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: St. James Property Co., Llcs Notice Of Motion To Vacate, Or In The Alternative To Correct, Final Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 18, 2023 line 1. ST. JAMES PROPERTY CO., LLC'S NOTICE OF MOTION TO VACATE, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE TO CORRECT, FINAL ARBITRATION AWARD is continued to February 22, 2023 for the moving party to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers. (Opposition and reply received). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TROY REESE, SUCCESSOR TRUSTTEE ET AL VS. MARTHA ALVAREZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Under Ccp 1008 Of Order Sustaining Demurrer Without Leave To Amend
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 18, 2023 line 4. PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION UNDER CCP 1008 OF ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND The Court will treat this motion as a motion for mandatory relief under CCP 473(b) based on counsel's mistake, i.e. failure to address the demurrer served via e-mail. The motion is granted. Defendant's demurrer shall be set for hearing on February 24, 2023. No later than January 27, 2023 Plaintiff to file an opposition to defendant's demurrer and deliver courtesy copies of the original demurrer and opposition to department 501 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date no later than January 31, 2023. Defendant shall file a reply, if any, no later than February 10, 2023. The Court notes that the notation November 10, 2022 ruling "no opposition filed" does not indicate that the demurrer is sustained because no opposition is filed. The notice is made to let the parties know that no compliance with CRC 3.1312 is required as the motion is unopposed. The Court considers the merits of the moving papers in all cases. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 18, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED in part and moot in part.
